after.detail: Increased structural length may exceed vibration test limits
Total mass may exceed accommodation envelope

new_cascade_issues.title: Increased structural length may exceed vibration test limits
new_cascade_issues.description: The change in dimensions could alter the modal frequencies and natural modes of vibration, potentially causing the structure to fail the random vibration test at 14.1 Grms.

new_cascade_issues.title: Thermal gradient margin may decrease
new_cascade_issues.description: Increasing the length of the base structure can increase thermal gradients across the optical bench, potentially violating the requirement for a maximum ΔT of 15°C during orbital cycles.

new_cascade_issues.title: Total mass may exceed accommodation envelope
new_cascade_issues.description: The change in dimensions could increase the overall mass of the payload, potentially exceeding the 35 kg limit.

new_cascade_issues.title: Shock test may need re-evaluation
new_cascade_issues.description: The change in dimensions could affect the shock response characteristics, necessitating a reanalysis of the structure's ability to withstand launch shock loads.

new_missing_tests.activity: Shock test per launcher SRS ICD
new_missing_tests.reason: The change in dimensions may affect the shock response characteristics, necessitating a re-evaluation of the structure's ability to withstand launch shock loads.

new_missing_tests.activity: Thermal balance test to validate TMM
new_missing_tests.reason: The change in dimensions may alter thermal gradients and heat dissipation, requiring a new thermal balance test.
